summary.overview:
- **Survey Data Improvements:** Exclude zero scores to prevent misleading participation trends; enhances report accuracy and reliability.

- **Benchmark Naming Clarity:** Standardize terms for manager and company benchmarks; improves score interpretation across organizational structures.

- **Custom Driver Settings:** Customers can save tailored driver visibility per survey type, increasing relevance and reducing analysis noise.

- **Scheduled Exports:** Introduced for lifecycle/custom surveys with flexible timing; enhances secure data accessibility for enterprise customers.

- **UI Enhancements:** Simplified filtering options and clearer column labels in reports; improves user experience and data clarity.

- **Feedback Module Translation:** Partial support for translating feedback into English; aids global customers in analyzing multilingual feedback.
summary.action_items:
**Karthik N**
Deliver priority render file by Monday evening with line-by-line context (01:11)
Implement dashboard enhancements for showing empty survey months as blanks on participation graphs (06:11)
Enable configuration to exclude unwanted drivers for future survey reports as per customer needs (14:17)
Finalize role-based access feature for managers and sub-admins to create and edit actions with appropriate restrictions (15:57)
Add company average and benchmark score columns to reports with clarifications on benchmark reliability (24:24)
Integrate partial English translation within feedback module (27:41)
Develop scheduled export functionality for lifecycle and continuous surveys, including frequency options and confidential data compliance (36:23)
Define lifecycle data access and separation protocols for enterprise customers (44:03)

**Shabharish V**
Support enhancements on participation dashboard improvements and line graph visualization discussion (04:40)
Assist with terminology changes and flexible settings for reporting benchmarks and driver visibility (09:05)
Support role-based permission mapping for managers and sub-admins (15:57)
Collaborate on report filtering and column labeling for driver and comment counts (23:46)
Verify scheduled export utility applicability for enterprise lifecycle service customers (38:42)

**Yugi P**
Verify usability of dashboard graphs including handling of zero vs empty scores in survey months without data (05:29)
Confirm terminology and filter presets in reporting modules (20:12)
Collaborate on feedback module translation and removing open feedback questions (27:38)
Evaluate removal of manager reaction feature from feedback email templates (30:21)
Support scheduled export feature definition including email delivery configurations and export frequencies (36:23)
summary.notes:
## **Survey Data and Benchmarking Enhancements**

The team decided to improve survey data representation and benchmarking to reflect realistic participation and comparison metrics.

- **Refined survey score handling to exclude zero scores when no survey was conducted** (08:13)  
    - The line graph will show empty data points instead of zeros for months without surveys  
    - This prevents misleading dips and spikes in participation and driver scores over time  
    - Shabharish emphasized removing zero scores from inactive months to improve data accuracy  
    - This change aligns visual data with actual survey activity, enhancing report reliability

- **Clarified company and manager benchmark naming conventions for better user understanding** (08:24)  
    - “Manager benchmark” will be referred to as “company overall score” for consistency  
    - Karthik noted multiple companies/organizations exist under one customer, so naming will adapt accordingly  
    - The benchmark naming will be flexible to reflect vertical or company-specific views as needed  
    - This helps users interpret scores appropriately based on organizational structure

- **Allowed customers to save customized driver visibility settings per survey type** (14:17)  
    - For example, customers can exclude certain drivers in pulse or Adula surveys if irrelevant  
    - This setting will apply platform-wide, not just individual customers like Yahoo  
    - Karthik highlighted this as a way to tailor driver metrics to each customer’s survey context  
    - It improves the relevance of driver scores and prevents noise in analysis

- **Expanded reporting features to add company average and benchmark score columns** (24:24)  
    - Yugi requested adding columns for company average and benchmark scores in reports  
    - Karthik explained benchmark data is imperfect but some companies provide question-wise benchmarks  
    - This enhancement will support better comparative insights in report dashboards  
    - It aids users in quickly assessing performance relative to company norms

## **Role-Based Access and Manager Interaction Controls**

Decisions were made to refine role-specific permissions and interaction capabilities to improve data security and user experience.

- **Implemented role-based restrictions on survey data actions for managers and sub-admins** (16:25)  
    - Managers and sub-admins can view results and conversations but cannot create or edit surveys  
    - Admins retain full control over survey creation and report exports  
    - This limits data modification rights to authorized roles, protecting survey integrity  
    - Karthik explained this maintains data quality while allowing managers meaningful visibility

- **Enabled managers to initiate conversations and create actions within permitted scopes** (15:08)  
    - Creation of actions and conversations is allowed for managers with appropriate permissions  
    - Admins and sub-admins have configurable options to grant or restrict these capabilities  
    - This facilitates direct manager involvement in feedback follow-up without compromising control  
    - Shabharish supported showing these actions clearly in the manager interface

- **Refined “My Company Score” terminology and participation visibility for indirect reporting managers** (18:26)  
    - The score label will be renamed to clarify it represents the manager’s company-level performance  
    - Participation metrics will reflect team-level involvement beneath indirect managers  
    - This improves clarity on whose data managers are seeing and responsible for  
    - It supports accurate tracking of engagement and performance within reporting lines

- **Removed manager reaction and certain feedback features to simplify email templates and feedback modules** (30:21)  
    - Manager reaction customization was rejected to avoid complexity in email communications  
    - Open feedback removal was discussed to streamline reporting pages  
    - These decisions focus on trimming less-used features to improve system usability  
    - Karthik confirmed these removals align with current customer needs and reduce maintenance

## **Scheduled Export and Lifecycle Survey Support**

The group prioritized scheduled exports as a critical feature for enterprise lifecycle survey customers, enhancing data accessibility and workflow.

- **Scheduled exports introduced for lifecycle and custom service surveys with flexible timing options** (35:53)  
    - Customers like Yahoo will get weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly export options via email  
    - This addresses the need to move confidential lifecycle survey data out of Culture Monkey securely  
    - Karthik highlighted the importance for hiring and manager performance metrics tracking  
    - Scheduled exports enable automated data delivery, supporting ongoing enterprise reporting needs

- **Access control and data separation managed carefully for lifecycle and custom service data exports** (44:08)  
    - Data attributes like employee ID, email, and name will be carefully segregated by service type  
    - Finance, operations, and other departments will maintain separated views within lifecycle and custom services  
    - This ensures privacy and compliance while allowing integrated reporting across teams  
    - Yugi and Karthik confirmed this approach meets enterprise data governance requirements

- **Platform-level eligibility controls ensure only authorized users access lifecycle and export features** (38:42)  
    - Users with platform access will gain appropriate rights to lifecycle data and export functions  
    - This prevents unauthorized data exposure and aligns with company security policies  
    - Export options will cover advanced and dynamic reports for comprehensive data delivery  
    - This builds a scalable export system supporting all enterprise customer needs

## **Language, Feedback Module, and UI Improvements**

The team focused on improving language support, feedback translation, and user interface refinements to enhance customer experience.

- **Partial implementation of feedback translation in the new feedback module, focusing on English** (27:38)  
    - Feedback will be translatable back to English for better analysis across languages  
    - Karthik noted full translation is partially possible currently and will expand over time  
    - This supports global customers receiving feedback in multiple languages  
    - Yugi emphasized this as a key improvement for actionable insights from diverse regions

- **Removal of low-priority or duplicate feedback questions and language inconsistencies** (26:07)  
    - Rejected some product language changes and repetitive questions to simplify surveys  
    - This cleanup improves clarity and reduces survey fatigue for respondents  
    - The team prioritized existing benchmarks over new low-impact features  
    - Karthik confirmed these removals align with customer feedback and product focus

- **UI adjustments include renaming columns and filtering options for clearer report views** (23:39)  
    - Columns for driver and comment counts will be labeled clearly in reports  
    - Filter options will restrict selection to single sets for more precise data views  
    - These changes help users interpret data faster and reduce confusion  
    - Yugi and Karthik collaborated to finalize these user-friendly enhancements

- **Dashboard and participation rate controls enhanced for better overtime analysis** (04:51)  
    - Participation dashboards now support driver scores over time with clearer control options  
    - This allows managers to monitor engagement trends and improve survey response rates  
    - Shabharish supported adding controls to exclude months without surveys from calculations  
    - These improvements help customers make timely, data-driven decisions on employee feedback programs
